name: Visigothic Kingdom
note: The Visigoths settled in southwestern Gaul (Aquitaine) as foederati in 418; after defeat by Clovis at Vouillé (507) they retreated to Iberia, making Toledo their capital. Converted from Arianism to Catholicism in 589 under Reccared — the defining event that integrated Visigothic and Hispano-Roman populations. Destroyed by the Muslim Umayyad invasion of 711.

figures.name: Reccared I
figures.significance: Converted the Visigothic kingdom from Arianism to Catholicism at the Third Council of Toledo (589) — unified Goths and Romans under one church; the most important act of Visigothic kingship

figures.name: Rodrigo (Roderic)
figures.significance: Last Visigothic king; defeated and killed at the Battle of Guadalete (711) by the Umayyad Muslim army under Tariq ibn Ziyad — the end of Visigothic Spain
